title: betula papyrifera subsp. occidentalis
intro: Betula papyrifera subsp. occidentalis, also known as the western paper birch, is a subspecies of paper birch (Betula papyrifera) that is native to the western regions of North America. The bark of this tree has been used for its medicinal properties, containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of western paper birch include reducing fever and relieving headaches, as well as treating conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. The bark can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves for topical use, and it can also be made into a powder for internal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
